title: Standings & statistics double-count overlapping Brasileirao seasons
evidence: internal/loader/loader.go:156,264 map both Brasileirao_Matches.csv and novo_campeonato_brasileiro.csv to competition "Brasileirao Serie A"; 2019 appears as a full 380-match season in BOTH files (grep: 380 rows each). internal/query/query.go:288 GetStandings and :370 GetStatistics iterate ds.Matches with NO deduplication (unlike FindMatches which dedups at query.go:158), so 2003-2019 Brasileirao points and total_matches are roughly doubled. TASK.md's example '2019 Brasileirao: Flamengo 90 pts' would render as ~180 pts.
suggestion: Deduplicate matches once at load time (or in the standings/statistics aggregation) by date+home+away+competition, the same key FindMatches already uses.

title: ATDD acceptance tests are not data-isolated per the prompt
evidence: prompts/ATDD.md requires each scenario start 'from a running but empty system and shares no data with other tests'; acceptance_test.go:31 newServer() builds every test against the real shipped CSVs (mcp.NewServer("data/kaggle")) and asserts on fixed real-world facts (e.g. acceptance_test.go:205 'Flamengo as 2019 champion', :140 'Neymar'). Tests are mutually independent (fresh server each) but are coupled to dataset contents, not an empty system.
suggestion: Either seed each test from a small fixed in-test fixture, or note explicitly that for a read-only dataset server the 'empty system' clause is satisfied by a fresh load per test.

title: Robustness beyond spec: multi-format dates, team-name normalization, extra competition mapping
evidence: internal/loader/loader.go:95 handles 5 date formats; :114 NormalizeTeam strips state suffixes (covers AC10 Palmeiras/Palmeiras-SP); :340 maps Copa Sulamericana even though not required; mcp/server.go:244 implements full JSON-RPC stdio MCP protocol (initialize/tools/list/tools/call) beyond the in-process Call interface the tests use.
suggestion: No action; recorded as positive signal for cross-run comparison.
